title: Protocol: per-capita clinical trial density across 30 African nations
body: This cross-sectional registry analysis will rank 30 African countries by interventional clinical trial density per million population using ClinicalTrials.gov as the data source. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for all interventional studies in each country using filter.advanced with AREA[StudyType]INTERVENTIONAL and query.locn for location, calculating trials per million with prespecified population denominators. Five global comparators (United States, United Kingdom, India, Brazil, China) will be queried identically for benchmarking. Each country will receive a trials-per-million value, a density classification as hub, moderate, desert, or void, and an Africa-specific rank. Secondary analyses will examine income-group stratification by World Bank classification, Spearman rank correlation between GDP per capita and trial density, regional breakdown across five sub-regions, and a population-weighted injustice metric quantifying the share of Africans in void or desert countries. All query logic and denominators will be archived for reproducibility. No patient-level data will be accessed. Limitations include reliance on a single registry and on external population estimates.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.name: methods before outcomes
checks.detail: API query method precedes outcome definition.
